t_ch: 当然啦，外人看我，就只能看到我在社交活动上光彩照人的模样，还有我在娱乐新闻上的各种劲爆头条…他们哪里知道我为了上头条，事先要排演多久那些傻事。
t_en: Of course, people only notice my glamorous appearances at social events and the mad buzz I create in the news... They're clueless about the rehearsals I have to do for these absurd moments, just so I can get on the headlines.

t_ch: 我才不怕被骂，只有有人还在关注我，那就意味着我还有钱赚…所以说，像我这样聪明漂亮脸皮又厚的女人…活该我赚得盆满钵满，哈哈哈！
t_en: I'm not bothered by the name-calling, because if I've still got followers, that means there's money to be made... So, being the smart, gorgeous, and shameless woman that I am, it only makes sense that I'm rolling in dough, hahaha!

t_ch: 我很好奇…
t_en: I'm very curious...

t_ch: 托马斯是谁？很厉害吗？
t_en: Who's Thomas? Is he a big deal?

t_ch: 托马斯是我丈夫，我们才结婚没多久。他在星际和平公司工作，经常能和一些大人物碰面，时常要负责一些…重要项目～
t_en: Thomas is my husband, and we just got married. He works at the Interastral Peace Corporation, mixing it up all the time with some high-flyers and leading a few... "important" projects.

t_ch: 我听他口气，连什么「托帕总监」他都认识，多少算是有点人脉吧～
t_en: It sounds like he even knows "Director Topaz" personally. I guess he's pretty well-connected.

t_ch: 托马斯年纪虽然比我大一些，但能娶到我这种级别的明星，他自己当然也得有点硬实力。
t_en: Thomas is a little older than me, but to land someone of my fame, he definitely holds his own.

t_ch: 谈谈你在空艇上的见闻呗…
t_en: Tell me about your time on the airship...

t_ch: 这艘空艇？简直是我的第二个家！顶级派对，各路名流，应有尽有——你永远不知道会在下一个转角遇见什么级别的大人物！
t_en: The airship? Oh, it's practically my second home! Think top-tier parties, everything you need at your fingertips, and elites from every field imaginable. You never know which bigshots you'll bump into around the corner!

t_ch: 不过呢，他们都将拜倒在我的美貌与名气下。真是拿他们没办法…谁让我生来就是该活在镁光灯下的呢～
t_en: But they all fawn over my beauty or fame. What can I say? I am simply destined to live under the spotlight!

t_ch: 你和知更鸟比，谁更有名？
t_en: Who's more famous, you or Robin?

t_ch: 我承认，还是知更鸟小姐更出名。我唱的都是电子舞曲，上的节目都是真人秀…我们领域不同，没什么可比较的，反正都很有名就是了。
t_en: I'll admit, Miss Robin is more famous than I am. My niche singing is electronic dance music and all the appearances I make are in reality shows... so it doesn't make much sense to compare us, who work in different fields. We're both famous in our own ways.

t_ch: 哎呀呀，你又不是小孩子，就别搞什么「人气排行榜」那一套了。名气这种东西，能拿来变现才是最重要的～
t_en: Oh, come on, are you a kid? Quit making this into a popularity contest. What really matters is using your fame to make money!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好了，亲爱的，我们就先聊到这儿吧。
t_en: It was great chatting with you, sweetie.

t_ch: 你瞧我这件怎么样？我身上这件可是限量版，羡慕吧～
t_en: How do you like my dress? It's a limited edition piece. Jealous?

t_ch: 好了，不逗你了，我们回头再聊。姐姐我要去参加下一场派对了，看我这身限量版…哼哼，不把其他住客迷死！
t_en: Haha, enough teasing. Catch you later! I've got another party to hit. Heh, all the other guests are totally going to go gaga over my limited-edition gown!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 你瞧我这件怎么样？我身上这件可是限量版，羡慕吧～
t_en: How do you like my dress? It's a limited edition piece. Jealous?

t_ch: 好了，不逗你了，我们回头再聊。姐姐我要去参加下一场派对了，看我这身限量版…哼哼，不把其他住客迷死！
t_en: Haha, enough teasing. Catch you later! I've got another party to hit. Heh, all the other guests are totally going to go gaga over my limited-edition gown!

t_ch: 咦，新面孔？很高兴认识你，我叫萨曼莎。呃…你是找我谈生意的话，无任欢迎，交朋友的话…那还是免谈吧。
t_en: Oh, a new face? Hi, I'm Samantha. Um... if you want to talk business, I'm all ears. But if it's friendship you're after, I'm afraid that's not on my agenda.

t_ch: 你好冷酷，你好无情…
t_en: Ouch, that's pretty harsh...

t_ch: 呵呵…我在这里是为了寻找能够改变游戏规则的商业机会，不是来交朋友的。
t_en: Haha, I'm on the hunt for game-changing business deals, not friendships.

t_ch: 闲聊几句也不行吗？
t_en: Not even a bit of chitchat?

t_ch: 那自然没问题。闲聊的话欢迎，但长期来往的话还是算了，我不想在人际交往上浪费太多时间精力。
t_en: Sure, small talk's fine. But long-term connections are a no-go. I'd rather not waste my time and energy on that.

t_ch: 投资是相当消耗脑力的事，社交也是…没什么事的话，请让我在这儿放空一会儿吧。
t_en: Investments and socializing both exhaust me... So, if there's nothing important, I'd appreciate some downtime.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 这里有什么商机？
t_en: What kinds of business ventures do they have around here?

t_ch: 有个很有前瞻性的项目，关系到匹诺康尼的未来。具体的细节属于商业机密，我不方便透露太多。
t_en: There's a promising project that's going to shape the future of Penacony. Unfortunately, it's still under wraps, so I can't divulge any details just yet.

t_ch: 不过你要是在星际和平公司认识人——我是说，某些关键部门里的员工——你可以去打听打听，说不定会有惊喜。
t_en: But if you have connections within the IPC, especially in key departments, asking around might bring you some exciting deets.

t_ch: 看来有很多人想跟你交朋友？
t_en: Sounds like people are vying to get onto your friend list, huh?

t_ch: 算是吧，你或许会在某些商业杂志的封面见过我。我的生活几乎被工作填满，有时候我会走到甲板上，独自看看空艇下方的风景。
t_en: Possibly. I've been on a few business magazine covers. Work pretty much fills my days, but sometimes I escape to the deck to enjoy the views, all by myself.

t_ch: 可惜，到目前为止，我独自看风景的时间加起来不超过五分钟。只能说…悲哀吧，呵呵。
t_en: Unfortunately, I can't even manage to get in five minutes of alone time to take in the views. Kind of sad, isn't it?

t_ch: 有遇到什么有趣的事吗？
t_en: Is there anything interesting going on?

t_ch: 前不久有个住客吃太撑被送去看医生了，我本想去考察下空艇上的医疗团队，却意外发现这里摆着不少价值连城艺术品……
t_en: A while back, a guest required medical attention for overeating. I meant to check out the airship's medical team, but I ended up stumbling upon the load of priceless artworks here.

t_ch: 这倒是激发了我投资艺术品的兴趣——啊，抱歉，在我看来这是很有趣的事，不过对于非投资领域的人，可能听起来就有点太过无聊了。
t_en: They piqued my interest in art investment. Oh, sorry, I realize what fascinates me might not be as intriguing to those outside the investment world.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯，正好让我静一静。
t_en: Perfect. I get some time to myself then.

t_ch: 噢对了，我们也算是投缘，那就免费送你一条投资箴言——
t_en: By the way, since we've gotten along so well, let me offer you a piece of free investment advice:

t_ch: 投资并不会带给你快乐的，它只是一个过程，无关其他。
t_en: Investing won't bring you happiness. It's nothing more than a means.

t_ch: 萨曼莎似乎很放松，但你注意到她的眼神还是一如初见那样犀利。
t_en: Samantha looks relaxed, yet you notice that her gaze is as sharp as when you first saw her.

t_ch: 市场就像大海，时有波动，总是充满不确定性…但这正是检验一个投资者洞察力的时刻。
t_en: The market's full of uncertainties, much like the unpredictable nature of the ocean's volatile waters... It's where an investor's keen observation is put to the test.

t_ch: 风浪总是伴随机会，关键是要能够从容不迫地分析形势，做出最合理的判断，就像现在这样——耐心…冷静…等候…唔，不知道公司的谈判进行到哪个环节了？
t_en: Fluctuations always bring opportunities. The key is to calmly assess the situation and arrive at the most rational decision, like what I'm doing now — waiting, but with patience and composure. Ugh, I wonder how the IPC negotiations went?

t_ch: 市场上好像还没有什么太大的动静…哼，看来还要再观望一阵子了。
t_en: Not much is happening in the market right now... Hmph, seems like I'll need to continue keeping a close watch.

t_ch: 萨曼莎没有说话，但你从她嘴角向下的弧度里读出了某种沉默的悲伤。
t_en: Samantha keeps silent, yet a quiet sorrow is discernible in the downturned corners of her mouth.

t_ch: 我的前期投入全都打了水漂…哎，真是倒霉。
t_en: All my initial investments have gone down the drain... Alas, such unfortunate luck.

t_ch: 或许外人眼里，投资就只有砸钱和挣钱两个动作，但对我而言…投资也算是某种情感的寄托了——虽然听起来很奇怪，但我感觉投资的过程跟养小孩没什么区别。
t_en: To outsiders, investing may just be about pumping in money and reaping the profits. But for me... feelings are also invested... Odd as it may sound, I see investment as akin to raising kids.

t_ch: 哎，明明前景这么好…害我抱有那么大的期望…现在却要闭店？呵，生意场如战场…或许这就是商业世界残酷的一面吧。
t_en: They were really promising before this, that's why I had high hopes for them... Who would've expected them to close shop? *sigh* Looks like the business world is indeed a battleground, and this is just one of its harsh sides.

t_ch: 萨曼莎啊萨曼莎，你是个成熟的投资人了，你要学会接受，并不是每一次投资都能得到回报……
t_en: Oh Samantha, as savvy an investor as you are, you must come to accept that not all investments yield returns...

t_ch: …哎，道理我都懂。就是不知道这次要花多少时间来消化这个噩耗……
t_en: ... *sigh* I do know that. I'm just not sure how long I'll take to recover from this setback this time...

t_ch: 这完全就是商业欺诈！
t_en: This is an outright business fraud!

t_ch: 商业投资，最重要的就是信任…我真不知道这种人是怎么能混到空艇上来的！
t_en: Trust is the cornerstone of business investment! I can't fathom how these individuals managed to get on board the airship!

t_ch: 你别以为这里来的都是什么社会名流…我跟你说，这里鱼龙混杂，诈骗犯可不会在脸上写明自己是骗子——名流？呸，就是个满嘴跑火车的混蛋！
t_en: Don't assume every guest here is a social elite! Trust me, this place attracts a mixed bag. Scammers don't come with labels on their foreheads! Elites? Bah! More like lie-spewing con artists!

t_ch: 发生什么事了？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 嗐，没什么，就是被我发现有个合作伙伴想空手套白狼…啧，可把我恶心坏了。
t_en: Ah, it's nothing major. I just caught a potential partner attempting to scam me into forking out their share of the capital, and it's left me thoroughly disgusted!

t_ch: 要我帮忙吗？
t_en: Do you need any help?

t_ch: 谢谢你的好意，不过我没什么实质性损失，暂时还不需要帮忙。如果真被骗钱，我肯定会报警的——你放心，猎犬们可不是软柿子。
t_en: Thanks. I didn't lose anything, so I'm fine. If I'd really been scammed, I'd have called the police. But don't stress it, the Bloodhounds aren't to be messed with.

t_ch: 现在有很多人自称什么很有名，还拿电视节目和废纸一样的成功学著作背书…我跟你说，压根不是这么回事——如果有人在你面前强调自己多出名，那你可要千万小心了！
t_en: These days, you'll find plenty of self-proclaimed celebrities, reciting mantras about how they're successful from TV shows and motivational books. But let me tell you, it's all fake! If someone tells you they're famous, tread carefully!

t_ch: 投资实体行业，好歹还能去现场看看，核实下情况。那种看不见摸不着的玩意，我奉劝你：以后要是碰到，可千万要小心！
t_en: When investing in brick-and-mortar businesses, you can drop by and see their situation for yourself. But with intangible investments, my advice? Be extra cautious!

t_ch: 太离谱了，我跟你说，在空艇上有些人自称什么名媛贵妇，又是什么明星名流，这里面水分可太大了！
t_en: It's absolutely shocking! You know, there are folks on the airship pretending to be socialites and celebrities. Turns out, it's all a sham!

t_ch: 我还以为上空艇来的都是有头有脸的大人物，没想到这种骗子也能混进去！真不知道家族是怎么做事的，居然放这种人进来！
t_en: I assumed everyone on the airship were reputable big shots. Never expected scammers to worm their way in! How did The Family even allow such people aboard!

t_ch: 我遇到的一个「女明星」——她自称的——极力劝我给她的娱乐公司投资，说什么收视率高，广告接不停，又有公司背书，如何如何厉害…呵，我差点信以为真。
t_en: I met a self-proclaimed "superstar" who tried to get me to invest in her entertainment company, touting high ratings, loads of commercial deals, and even an endorsement from the IPC... Pfft, I nearly fell for it.

t_ch: 还好我做事够谨慎，没评估出实际价值之前绝不会投钱。果然，最后被我发现了：什么狗屁公司，压根就是她老公开的皮包公司，旗下只有她一个人，这不摆明了是诈骗嘛！
t_en: Good thing I always do my due diligence and never rush into investments. It turned out that their "company" was just a facade owned by her husband, with her as the only artist on board. How's that not a scam!

t_ch: 萨曼莎当着你的面看了看手机，也不知道她究竟看了些什么，你只见她盯着屏幕笑容不止。
t_en: Samantha glances at her phone and breaks out into a lasting smile, making you curious about the happy news that she's reading.

t_ch: 哈哈！我就知道，我的眼光不会错的！
t_en: Haha, I knew it! My instincts are always right!

t_ch: 足足等了三年啊，这个项目终于营利了！连本带利翻了不少倍，哈哈，谁让当时去参加路演的其他投资人都是胆小鬼，只有我敢投呢！
t_en: After three years! This project finally hit profit, multiplying my investment along with its interest! Haha, too bad for the others who balked at the roadshow back then. I was the only one bold enough to jump in!

t_ch: 成功永远属于那些敢于梦想、勇于追求的人。三年时间，我看着他们公司从高层换人到经营方向大改…嗐，这一路上真是太艰难了。
t_en: Success is always reserved for the those who dare to dream big. Over three years, I've seen their company undergo leadership changes and major business pivots... I guess they've really been through a challenging journey.

t_ch: 还好，他们肯虚心接纳我的意见…现在我可以骄傲地说，在投资这一行，我就从没失手过！
t_en: Fortunately, they were open to my suggestions. Now, I can proudly say that I've never lost on an investment!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好，下次再聊吧，正好我也有点累了，想在这儿放空一会儿。
t_en: Let's catch up later. I'm feeling a bit tired at the moment and could really use some alone time.

t_ch: 噢对了，我们也算是投缘，那就免费送你一条投资箴言——
t_en: By the way, since we've gotten along so well, let me offer you a piece of free investment advice:

t_ch: 投资并不会带给你快乐的，它只是一个过程，无关其他。
t_en: Investing won't bring you happiness. It's nothing more than a means.

t_ch: 嗨嗨，别再盯着我了。招标的事得按流程走，公司的流程你懂的，一切公事公办。
t_en: Hey, could you stop staring at me? You know the bidding needs to follow the IPC's protocol. Business is business, after all.

t_ch: 我不是来投标的。
t_en: I'm not here about the bidding.

t_ch: 我只是个路人。
t_en: I'm just passing through.

t_ch: 噢噢，抱歉，我把你也当成那帮小老板了。
t_en: Oh, my bad. I took you for one of those bosses.

t_ch: 那自我介绍一下吧：我叫托马斯，是星际和平公司的员工，也是女明星艾丽卡的老公兼经纪人。公司派我出差，我就带着她来顺便度个蜜月。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself then. I'm Thomas, an IPC employee and manager of my celebrity wife, Erica. The IPC sent me here on a business trip, and I saw it as the perfect opportunity to double it as our honeymoon trip.

t_ch: 你也是住客吗？难得有缘相见，你要是有什么不懂，可以问我——哈哈，我可是仔细研究过「{TEXTJOIN#87}」的！
t_en: Are you a guest here as well? Since fate has us crossing paths, feel free to ask me anything. I've done my homework on {TEXTJOIN#87}!

t_ch: 慌什么！天塌了有总监顶着呢。
t_en: What's there to worry about! Even if the sky falls, the director will hold it up!

t_ch: 朋友，你得跟我一样淡定，才能适应各种压力。
t_en: My friend, you need to stay as calm as I am to handle all kinds of pressures out there.

t_ch: 就算是天塌了项目完蛋了，在我之上还有一堆总监之类的高层顶着。要是梦醒了…嘿，梦醒了那不就继续上班呗。
t_en: Even if the projects go sideways, there are plenty of bigwigs like the director to keep things afloat. And if the dream ends? Well, I'll just keep punching the clock then!

t_ch: 我想想，我有份不错的工作…嗯，还有当我老婆经纪人的副业，有个刚结婚的漂亮老婆…我啥都有，日子还长着呢，一步步来，什么都会有的。
t_en: Let's see, I've got a well-paying job, I double as my wife's manager, and I'm married to a beautiful woman. I really do have it all. And with so much life ahead of me, the best is yet to come.

t_ch: 在这个世界上，只有聪明人才能生存下来，而我，无疑是其中之一。
t_en: In this world, only the smart survive, and I am undoubtedly one of them.

t_ch: 可惜…我这辈子恐怕就到头了。
t_en: Sadly, it feels like I may be reaching the end of my time.

t_ch: 我们同事之间总爱讲一句话：「战略投资部只认功劳，不认苦劳」。哎，年纪越大，我就越感到一种莫名的悲哀。
t_en: There's a saying at work: "The Strategic Investment Department only recognizes credits, not efforts." *sigh* The older I get, the more that hits home.

t_ch: 以前，我压根没时间停下来思考这些玩意。只能使劲干活，努力往前爬…但我再怎么努力，力气终归有限。现在我老咯，很多事都感觉力不从心……
t_en: Before, I just worked hard and climbed the ladder without a second thought, even though there was only so much I could do. But now that I'm older, some things are no longer within my reach.

t_ch: …功劳越来越少，苦劳越来越多咯。所以比起公司的项目，我现在更在意的是副业…哎，艾丽卡花钱大手大脚的，真不知道在退休前能不能靠她那个公司捞够钱……
t_en: I'm putting in more effort but getting recognized for a lot less. That's why I'm more focused on my other job now. It's just that Erica's a big spender, so I'm not sure if I can make enough with her company before I retire.

t_ch: …在这个世界上只有不断赚钱才能不断前进，才能活下去…可惜啊，我老人家的生命已经要到头了，或许我这辈子都不能达到总监那个职级了吧……
t_en: In this world, you have to keep making money to move forward and survive... It's sad to think my life is nearing its end and I might never get to hold the title of director...

t_ch: 哎…不甘心啊，人和人之间的差别怎么就这么大呢？
t_en: *sigh* It's so unfair how people end up leading such different lives!

t_ch: 哼，连「交易」原则都搞不懂的蠢货，有什么资格来我面前晃悠！
t_en: Hmph, an idiot who doesn't even understand the principles behind making deals. What right do they have to strut in front of me?

t_ch: 有些小年轻仗着自己家世厉害，来跟我谈合作居然不谈报酬…太好笑了，怎么会有这样的蠢货！
t_en: Some rich kids try to talk deals with me without going into my cut of the earnings... That's just ridiculous! How can there be fools like that?

t_ch: 「交易」的原则是互惠互利，连打点我这种事都做不到，还想谈交易？我呸！
t_en: Trading is all about mutual benefits. They don't even think to pay me, and they hope to get themselves a deal? Bah!

t_ch: 别跟我说扯什么「年轻人不吃这一套」，我老人家还不吃这一套呢！装什么铁面无私职场清醒…那成，想求我？到我老婆那儿排队吃屁去吧！
t_en: Don't give me that "young people don't buy this" crap. We older folks aren't buying it either! To heck with that impartial and ethical facade! Want to beg me? They can line up to kiss my wife's behind!

t_ch: 做生意不懂人情世故？那还做什么生意！我都暗示得这么明显了还不懂…那就不能怪我老人家不留情面了！
t_en: If they don't get the ways of the world, they're not cut out for business! I've dropped plenty of hints, and they still haven't caught on! Don't blame me for being ruthless, then!

t_ch: 公司派我来这儿出差…这就是器重我的证明！
t_en: The IPC sending me on this business trip is a clear recognition of my value!

t_ch: 这艘空艇比我想得还要豪华，不愧是度假胜地匹诺康尼！这次带艾丽卡来果然没错，还能省下一大笔……
t_en: This airship is far more luxurious than I anticipated! No wonder Penacony is such a popular vacation spot! Bringing Erica along was definitely the right move, especially when I got to save a ton of money...

t_ch: 你也太抠门了吧？
t_en: Isn't that a bit cheap of you?

t_ch: 这算哪门子抠门，匹诺康尼…还是「{TEXTJOIN#87}」，多少人花重金都求不来的地方呢！
t_en: I'm not cheap! Penacony and {TEXTJOIN#87} aren't accessible to just anyone, even those with plenty of money!

t_ch: 艾丽卡不会有什么不满吗？
t_en: Won't Erica take issue with this?

t_ch: 你不懂，我才是这个家的核心！公司安排我来出差，一切当然是以我的事业为重啦。
t_en: You don't understand. I make the decisions in my family! Since the IPC sent me on a business trip, my career takes priority!

t_ch: 人生就是一场大型的棋局，只要有利可图，谁都能当棋子——你知道我为什么会找艾丽卡当我的结婚对象了吗？
t_en: Life is like a huge chessboard where, if there's profit to be made, anyone can be a pawn. Do you see why I picked Erica as my wife now?

t_ch: 她小有名气，出身很差，头脑简单，但靠着真人秀算是有点名气的。名气，就是名利场的入场券。至于什么出歌出碟的，那些都是不重要的点缀。
t_en: She's a simple-minded nobody from a humble family, but going on reality shows brought her reputation — her ticket to fame and fortune. Music, albums, and the whatnot are just frivolous add-ons to her portfolio.

t_ch: 可能有些人会觉得我下作，但我只是在更有效地整合、利用资源。公司这么器重我，给我这么好的发展机会，我怎么能浪费呢？
t_en: Some might think I'm cutting corners, but I'm just efficiently utilizing resources. How could I squander the trust and opportunity the IPC has given me?

t_ch: 托马斯朝你眨了眨眼。一道思绪刺入你的脑海，似有若无，转瞬即逝。
t_en: Thomas winks at you, and a thought flits through your mind, elusive and fleeting.

t_ch: 哈哈，我就喜欢和你这样的聪明人聊天。
t_en: Haha, I really enjoy chatting with smart folks like you.

t_ch: 来「{TEXTJOIN#87}」上的都是各界名流，我和艾丽卡居然能来这儿…真是做梦也想不到。
t_en: All guests on {TEXTJOIN#87} are elites from various fields. I never imagined Erica and I would be among them.

t_ch: 光是看到艇上皮沙发的牌子，我就知道这次出差是来对了。哈哈，真是多亏了公司对我的器重哇。
t_en: Just seeing the brand of the leather sofa confirms this business trip was the right choice — haha, all thanks to the IPC's trust in me.

t_ch: 幸亏带了艾丽卡，有她给我撑场面，加班都倍儿有面子，状态也比以前好了不少！就好像年轻了十岁…不，二十岁！
t_en: Thank goodness I brought Erica along. With her here to amp up my status, putting in extra work hours becomes something to gloat over. Heck, I even feel much better than before! It's as though I've become ten... no, twenty years younger!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 你老婆是明星？
t_en: Your wife's a celebrity?

t_ch: 当然，她是现在那个最火的真人秀的特邀嘉宾。我还给她出过碟呢…嘿嘿，我可是给她花重金开了个娱乐公司的，旗下就只签了她一个。
t_en: That's right. She's the special guest in that reality show that's been making its rounds everywhere. I even released an album for her... Heh, I really went all in for her, going as far as to start an entertainment company, with her as the only talent.

t_ch: 公司派你来干什么的？
t_en: What did the IPC send you here for?

t_ch: 哈，参与项目的可都是大人物…这种机密你还是别乱问了，我可不想被公司保安拎着丢出去。
t_en: Hah, everyone on this project's a big shot... Let's keep classified information off limits. Getting escorted out by IPC security isn't exactly something on my bucket list.

t_ch: 度蜜月也要出差，这么拼？
t_en: Working even during your honeymoon? You're a workaholic, huh?

t_ch: 呃，公司计划有变，我收到命令就赶过来处理。其实托帕总监是建议我继续放婚假来着，不过我觉得去哪儿度假都是去，还不如来匹诺康尼呢。
t_en: Uh, the IPC had a change of plans, so I came on immediate notice to work on them. Director Topaz actually said to go ahead with my marriage leave, but I figured a vacation anywhere's still a vacation, so why not Penacony?

t_ch: 再说了，「{TEXTJOIN#87}」上啥都有，奢华无比，排场十足，带艾丽卡过来享受一下也挺好的，工作生活两不误嘛。这里名流多，我还能替她多接几个通告呢。
t_en: Besides, everything can be found on {TEXTJOIN#87}. It'd be nice to bring Erica here, to soak up the grand and beyond luxurious vibe. Who's to say I can't juggle both work and life? Plus, with so many bigwigs here, I might even be able to land her some gigs.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好，回头再聊吧。
t_en: Alright, talk to you later.

t_ch: 真是没办法啊，你说这人被器重就是…公司没我不行啊！但既然公司这么看得起我，我也得投桃报李嘛。
t_en: There's no helping it. I guess being held in such high regard means the IPC can't function without me! I mean, if they think so highly of me, I can only live up to it.

t_ch: 不说了，我得继续工作了，还有一大堆邮件没看呢。
t_en: Alright, time to get back to work. There's a ton of mail I have to read and respond to.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好，回头再聊吧，毕竟我眼下也不是很有空。
t_en: Alright, talk to you later. I do have work to get to anyway.

t_ch: 真是没办法啊，你说这人被器重就是…公司没我不行啊！但既然公司这么看得起我，我也得投桃报李嘛。
t_en: There's no helping it. I guess being held in such high regard means the IPC can't function without me! I mean, if they think so highly of me, I can only live up to it.

t_ch: 不说了，我得继续工作了，还有一大堆邮件没看呢。
t_en: Alright, time to get back to work. There's a ton of mail I have to read and respond to.

t_ch: 你好，如你所见，我是位律师，来这儿出差。很遗憾，我不能谈论任何工作细节，基于「公司」的保密原则，你懂的…但我想我们可以换个话题——
t_en: Hello. As you can tell, I'm a lawyer, here on a business trip. I'm sure you already know, but regrettably, I cannot discuss any work details under the confines of the IPC's confidentiality agreements. If you wish to talk about something else though...

t_ch: 看你的模样，应该不是匹诺康尼本地人吧。需要法律援助可以随时找我，只要是在法律允许的范围内——好吧，或者稍微越界一点点，我都能帮你搞定。
t_en: Hmm, you don't seem to be a native Penaconian. Feel free to come to me if you require legal assistance, as long as it's permissible by the law... Alright, scratch that. You can count on me, even if it's bordering on gray areas.

t_ch: 有没有工作以外的话题？
t_en: Do you talk about anything else other than work?

t_ch: 有。但很抱歉，我可不是来匹诺康尼度假的。
t_en: I do, but sorry to disappoint, pal. I did not come to Penacony for a vacation.

t_ch: 你真是个工作狂。
t_en: You're a true-blue workaholic.

t_ch: 时间就是金钱。但如果你愿意无偿捐赠我一笔钱，我也可以当场度假。
t_en: Time is money. Of course, if you're willing to unconditionally offer me a sum of money, I can switch right into vacation mode.

t_ch: 干我们这行，如果你不能保持冷静，那你就已经输了。
t_en: In our line of work, you lose the moment you fail to keep your cool.

t_ch: 要记住，不动声色才是在法庭上取胜的王道——就像我现在这样。
t_en: Remember, maintaining your composure — like the way I'm behaving now — is the golden rule to winning in court.

t_ch: 我的老朋友曾经这么评价过我：「麦吉尔，你在法庭上就像是只猫，总是灵活又冷静。」这就是猫科动物为什么杀伤力如此之大。
t_en: An old friend once said this about me, "McGill, you're so calm and quick on your feet in court, almost like the human embodiment of a cat." This is also why felines are so deadly, by the way.

t_ch: 我接过的案子比你吃过的面包还多——我的对手也是。从法律界的巨鲨到街头混混…当全世界都在对你喊打喊杀时，保持冷静就变成了一门技艺。
t_en: I've taken on more cases than all the shoelaces you've tied, and so have my opponents. From the bigwigs of the legal profession to the street thugs... Basically, when the whole world calls for your death, remaining calm becomes an art.

t_ch: 所幸，我的技艺出神入化…所以我总能在最关键时刻找到出路。
t_en: Fortunately, I'm already a master of the art... That's how I always manage to find a way out at the most critical juncture.

t_ch: 呵…很多人都对律师抱有偏见，认为我们是冷血动物。
t_en: Hmph, many people out there hold a prejudiced view about lawyers, considering us to be cold-blooded animals.

t_ch: 有时候，你明知道当事人并不清白，但你还是得给对方当狗。我刚参加工作的时候就常常在想…世界上真的有所谓的正义吗？
t_en: There are times when your client is obviously guilty, but you're still required to go all out to represent and defend them. When I first started my job, I couldn't stop asking myself if justice truly exists in this world.

t_ch: 后来，我告诫自己，工作的事归工作，不要太投入。不然我不去工作，事务所上下这么多人，我的家人，谁来养活呢？
t_en: Later on, I made it a point to compartmentalize my work and to never get overly invested in it. What else could I do? If I were to stop working, who's going to pay the employees at the law firm? How's my family going to survive?

t_ch: 可惜…说归说，但做不做得到又是另外一回事了。当你看到客户的眼泪时，你就会明白…在我们漫长的人生里，有许多思想斗争其实根本没必要。
t_en: Sadly, talk is cheap. Actually practicing it is a whole other business. When clients come to you in tears, you realize that most of the ideological struggles you spent all your life being conflicted over are in fact meaningless.

t_ch: 那些家伙脑子里是不是进了水…？！
t_en: Are those guys soft in the head!?

t_ch: 你知道吗？这个世界上最恶心的事，并不是有罪的人逍遥法外——而是看到自己的客户畏手畏脚，活得像个受气包！
t_en: You know what? The worst feeling in the world isn't watching criminals get off scot-free, but seeing your client living their life gripped by fear, becoming no different from a doormat!

t_ch: 所以，我一直以来都会告诫自己的每一位客户，要准备好面对一切后果，更别怕脏了自己的手。哎，只可惜…并不是所有人都能听得进去。
t_en: That's why I've always been reminding my clients to be prepared for any consequence, even if it means having to get their hands dirty. *sigh* It's a pity my advice doesn't sit well with everyone.

t_ch: 碰到这种客户，我就感觉自己像是跟整个世界为敌——想想吧，连你的客户都不信任你该有多伤士气！
t_en: With such clients, it's as though I'm fighting against the world. What if it were you? Can you imagine how demoralizing it is when your own client refuses to trust you?

t_ch: 人活得这么憋屈就算了，到开庭的时候、为自己争取权益的时候也这么憋屈…那还找我当律师干嘛？！真是搞不懂！
t_en: I have no say if they're fine with living an oppressed life, but when they let everyone walk all over them in court — a place where they can fight for their own rights... Is there even a point in hiring me!? I can never get what they're thinking!

t_ch: 哈哈，匹诺康尼这个地方真是有趣…遍地是宝藏。
t_en: Haha, Penacony's an interesting place indeed... There's valuable information everywhere I go.

t_ch: 本以为只是个普通出差，没想到能遇到这么有趣的事。匹诺康尼这地方，还有这艘空艇，没想到背后藏了这么多复杂的秘密…哈哈，还好我心细。
t_en: I thought this was nothing more than an unremarkable work trip. Who would've expected to learn something so interesting? But for Penacony and this airship to be hiding such intricate secrets... Haha, I guess it's a good thing I'm meticulous.

t_ch: 我喜欢这种消耗脑力的活儿。根据我手头上的信息，无论谈判成功与否，我都能为自己留一手。
t_en: I enjoy intellectual challenges like this. With the information I have at hand, I'll be able to emerge unscathed no matter how the negotiation turns out.

t_ch: 方便透露一二吗？
t_en: Can you divulge a thing or two?

t_ch: 别想套我话，我可不想惹上什么麻烦。
t_en: Don't try to trick me into revealing anything. I don't want to get into trouble.

t_ch: 你不怕公司报复你吗？
t_en: Aren't you worried about the IPC getting back at you?

t_ch: 只要我嘴巴够严，我不说，又有谁知道我知道呢？
t_en: As long as I keep my lips sealed, who's going to know what I know?

t_ch: 别误会，我不是想威胁公司。我只是喜欢这种仿佛在牌局里看清别人手牌的感觉。
t_en: Don't misunderstand me. I'm not trying to blackmail the IPC. I just enjoy the revelation I get when I discover what tricks people have up their sleeves.

t_ch: 这让我想起了我为什么会选择当律师——在复杂的世界中找到真相，然后利用这些真相为自己和客户赢得优势，这不是很有趣吗？
t_en: This reminds me of why I decided to become a lawyer: To seek out the truths in this complicated world, and use them to me and my client's advantage. Do you find that fun too?

t_ch: 最开始，我只是想更好地为我的重要客户服务，所以得了解这地方的所有细节。
t_en: Right at the start, I only dug into the details of this place so that I can better serve my VIP clients.

t_ch: 但和其他星球没什么不同…在所有光鲜亮丽的表面下，都有它的阴暗面，匹诺康尼也不例外。
t_en: Just as how the other planets have a dark side beneath their welcoming and dazzling appearance, the same holds true for Penacony.

t_ch: 而当我揭开这层面纱时…那种满足感，简直像亲手从地里掘出了金子。
t_en: And when I lift this veil, I get a sense of satisfaction no less than digging up gold from the ground myself.

t_ch: 哈哈，我从来不认为自己是个英雄，也不是什么恶棍。我只是一个律师，一个擅长收集一切有利信息的律师——未雨绸缪是我的职责。
t_en: Haha, I've never thought of myself as a hero, nor a villain. I'm just a lawyer, one who's honed a craft in gathering useful information. After all, it is my duty to be ahead of the game.

t_ch: 我很好奇…
t_en: I'm very curious...

t_ch: 你在做什么项目？
t_en: What case are you working on?

t_ch: 我只能说，我来这儿是提供一些法律咨询服务的，除此以外的内容…我很抱歉，基于「公司」的保密原则，恕我不能透露。
t_en: I can only say that I'm here to provide legal advice. I'm bound by the IPC's confidentiality agreement, so I'm afraid I can't tell you anything more.

t_ch: 能跟我介绍下你的业务吗？
t_en: Can you tell me a little about your work?

t_ch: 我的业务？你可以去打听打听，无论是什么类型的案件，从逃税罚单到重罪辩护，没有我接不了的案子。
t_en: My work? You can try asking around, I guess. No matter the case, be it tax evasion or felony defense, there isn't one that I won't take up.

t_ch: 但我最擅长的，是那种看起来无解的案子——其他律师一看就头疼的那种。对我来说，挑战越大，越能证明我的价值。
t_en: My expertise, though, lies in cases that seem hopeless. These are cases that other lawyers find torturous, but to me, the greater the challenge, the stronger the testament to my abilities.

t_ch: 你在法律界很有名吗？
t_en: Are you famous in the legal profession?

t_ch: 噢，你可能在我的同行口中听说过我的名字。我姑且算是这一行的「老油条」了吧，哈哈。
t_en: Oh, you might have heard of me from the other lawyers. I guess I can be considered a seasoned veteran in this field, haha.

t_ch: 各种千奇百怪的案子我都接过，而我每一次都能让客户满意。当然…这就是为什么我的咨询费比其他同行高出一大截。
t_en: I've taken on all sorts of strange cases and every single time, my clients walk away satisfied. Of course, this explains why my consultation fees are a lot higher than other fellow lawyers.

t_ch: 你对匹诺康尼感觉如何？
t_en: How do you find Penacony?

t_ch: 很有趣的问题。匹诺康尼有一种独特的魅力，但所有美丽的东西都有自己的阴暗面。权力斗争，美梦背后的秘密…「暗流涌动」应该是最能概括匹诺康尼的词了。
t_en: Interesting question. Well, Penacony has a unique charm, but all things glamorous are known to have a dark side. Power struggles and secrets behind sweet dreams... Perhaps "surging undercurrents" is the term that can best describe Penacony.

t_ch: 对于这次「出差」，虽然我不能跟你透露工作细节，但我可以告诉你，这是一次相当有趣的经历。只能说，在我看来，未来的匹诺康尼…商机无限！
t_en: I may not be able to share details of my work trip this time, but I can tell you that it was a rather interesting experience. Let me put it this way, Penacony will soon welcome never-ending business opportunities, at least the way I see it.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 抱歉，我还有些法律事务要处理，我们下次再聊。
t_en: Excuse me, but I have some legal matters to see to. Let's talk again sometime.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 抱歉，我还有些法律事务要处理，我们下次再聊。
t_en: Excuse me, but I have some legal matters to see to. Let's talk again sometime.

t_ch: 啾！是你呀客官…真是有活力呢，人家总能在美梦小镇各个角落见到你啾！
t_en: Tweet! It's you again... You're quite energetic, aren't you? I've seen you all across Dreamville!

t_ch: 客官也收到叶子店长的邀请了吗？怎么样，这艘豪华游艇是不是特别棒啾~
t_en: Have you also received Shopkeeper Leaf's invitation? How about it, huh? Isn't this luxury airship absolutely amazing? Tweet~

t_ch: 特别棒啾~
t_en: Yes, indeed! Tweet~

t_ch: 啾——讨厌，不要学人家说话啾！
t_en: Tweet... Don't be annoying! Stop talking like me, tweet!

t_ch: 说起来，叶子店长特别喜欢这艘游艇，为了建造它，可是花费了好多闪亮亮的石头啾！
t_en: Speaking of which, Shopkeeper Leaf really loves this airship. In order to construct it, he had to spend many glittering stones, tweet!

t_ch: 叶子店长？
t_en: Shopkeeper Leaf?

t_ch: 是的啾！叶子店长特别喜欢这艘游艇，为了建造它，可是花费了好多闪亮亮的石头啾！
t_en: Yes, tweet! Shopkeeper Leaf really loves this airship. In order to construct it, he had to spend many glittering stones, tweet!

t_ch: 看看这些精挑细选的树枝、色泽艳丽的石子…虽然品味尚不及人家，但也相当不错了啾！
t_en: Look at these carefully selected twigs and gorgeous stones... Although his taste might not be the best, it's still better than most people's, tweet!

t_ch: 对了客官，您会出现在人家面前，就说明我们肯定特别有缘…既然如此，人家有个小小的不情之请……
t_en: Right, since you have appeared before me, it means that we were fated to meet each other. If that's the case, I have a small favor to ask of you...

t_ch: 当然没问题。
t_en: Of course!

t_ch: 客官果然是大好人啾！人家的心跳都忍不住快了好几拍啾……
t_en: You're a nice person indeed! My heart couldn't help but beat faster at your response, tweet...

t_ch: 请恕我拒绝。
t_en: Please allow me to refuse.

t_ch: 哎呀~您还没问是什么请求呢，万一这件事只有您能帮忙呢？客官不要这么狠心啾~
t_en: Come on, you haven't even heard my request yet. What if you're the only one who can resolve this issue? Please don't be so heartless, tweet~

t_en: Is there any meaning to your name?

t_ch: 因为…以前我总喜欢当着客人的面喝苏乐达，久而久之就给大家造成了「原来狗狗也可以喝苏乐达」这样的错误印象，汪。
t_en: That's because I was always drinking SoulGlad in front of our guests. Over time, people formed the misconception that "dogs can drink SoulGlad as well," woof.

t_ch: 所以我就给自己起了这个名字，以免再有意外发生，汪。
t_en: I chose this name to prevent such accidents from occurring again, woof.

t_ch: 你有什么推荐的饮料吗？
t_en: Do you have any drinks to recommend?

t_ch: 好问题，汪！苏乐达我推荐买玻璃瓶装的，瓶身最好冰冻过，气泡绵密，喝起来才够冰爽，汪。
t_en: Great question, woof! I recommend getting the bottled SoulGlad, best if it has been chilled. The flurry of bubbles makes it extra refreshing, woof!

t_ch: 能给我打折吗？
t_en: Can you give me a discount?

t_ch: 现在在搞活动，已经是最低价了，不能再低了，汪！
t_en: We're having a promotion now, so it's already at the lowest possible price. I can't lower it any further, woof!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，汪。那下次再聊，汪！
t_en: Alright, woof. Let's talk another time, woof!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 饮料虽好，但宠物不能喝，小朋友不能多喝，汪。客人您也要注意不要过量饮用，汪！
t_en: Our drinks may be good, but pets mustn't have them and kids shouldn't drink too much either, woof. You should also note how much you're drinking so you don't overindulge, woof!

t_ch: 贵客光临，汪！客人您好，苏乐达最近搞活动，想来点吗？汪！
t_en: Welcome, esteemed guest! Woof! SoulGlad is currently running a promotion! Would you like to get some? Woof!

t_ch: 你尝试用钟表把戏触碰宠勿饮的内心，但无论表盘如何转动，你发现它都不为所动。
t_en: You attempt to rouse emotions from Soda Pop Pup with Clockwork, but no matter how much the dial moves, you realize that it remains unfazed.

t_ch: 宠勿饮将全副身心都扑在工作上，它永远只在乎如何用苏乐达讨好每一位顾客。
t_en: Soda Pop Pup is deeply engrossed in its work, only ever concerned with how it can delight every guest with SoulGlad.

t_ch: 真是个乖狗狗，像它这样心思澄澈的好孩子已经不多见了。
t_en: What a good dog. Well-behaved beings with pure minds are a dying breed.

t_ch: 船长周边，限量发售，先到先得！
t_en: Captain's merchandise available for a limited time! First come, first served!

t_ch: 欢迎光临…咦？你是…新任船长？船长好！
t_en: Welcome... Eh? You're the... new captain? Hi, captain!

t_ch: 你在卖我的周边？
t_en: You're selling my merchandise?

t_ch: 这个是咱们这儿的老传统啦，我这小店里除了零食，还卖船长的周边商品，可抢手啦。
t_en: It's a tradition we've had since forever here. Other than snacks, our store also sells the captain's merchandise — these are selling like hot cakes.

t_ch: 你怎么知道我是船长？
t_en: How can you tell I'm the captain?

t_ch: 您真幽默，要是我连船长都认不出来，我还卖什么船长周边啊。
t_en: Oh, you're a humorous one! I sell the captain's merchandise, you know? How can I not recognize you?

t_ch: 船长您来得正好，供应商刚刚送了一批新货过来，都是照足您的模样定制的小玩意。
t_en: Oh, captain! Just in time. Our supplier just delivered a new batch of goods, little knick-knacks designed after you.

t_ch: 虽说目前还处于预售阶段，但顾客们实在太热情，把未来一整年的出货量都给包圆了。真是拿他们没办法…您瞧，就是这款——
t_en: Well, they're still in the pre-sale phase, but the customers are so excited about them that they've ordered one whole year's worth of merchandise. They really know how to surprise me, huh? Look, this is what they ordered—

t_ch: 这是…照着我的模样做的？
t_en: This is... designed after me?

t_ch: 是的，说是能凸显您风趣幽默的特点，又保留您特有的纯真感。
t_en: That's right. The design is supposed to dramatize your humorous nature, while retaining the special innocence about you.

t_ch: …好像也没跟我打过招呼。
t_en: ...I don't think anyone checked in with me about this.

t_ch: 啊？不会吧？我记得苜蓿草那边是有和星穹列车交接的啊。我们这边是确认拿到了星穹列车方的同意才启动的。
t_en: Huh? Really? I know the Alfalfa was in liaison with the Astral Express. We even waited for the Astral Express to green-light it before going into production.

t_ch: …啊，我知道了，会不会是您贵人多忘事。先前交接的手续比较多，可能忘了……
t_en: ...Ah, maybe you've been so busy that you forgot all about it? Yeah, there was a ton of things in the handover, so that's likely the case...

t_ch: 我们给您单独留了一套以作您成为船长的贺礼。
t_en: We saved a set for you as a celebratory gift for your appointment as captain.

t_ch: 还有就是，我悄悄跟您说，麻烦您别声张——这套周边太抢手啦，我怕别的顾客听了不高兴——我给您留了一大箱，您可以敞开来买，不限量，价格的话就内部拿货价算。
t_en: Also, just between us: I saved a large crate, so you can purchase however many you like, all at an internal staff discount. But please don't tell anyone else about it. Our customers are already fighting to get their hands on this merchandise set. I wouldn't want to incur their wrath.

t_ch: 您还需要点别的什么吗？我在这儿值班，如果你有任何需求，随时喊我就好啦！
t_en: Is there anything else you need? I'm on duty here, so just call for me if you require anything!

t_ch: 你这儿都卖些什么？
t_en: What have you got for sale here?

t_ch: 零嘴和周边，都是空艇上的特色产品，享誉匹诺康尼的时尚单品。
t_en: Airship specialties: Snacks and merchandise. These are also trending fashion pieces on Penacony!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 你的名字是怎么回事？
t_en: What's up with your name?

t_ch: 哦这个呀…因为以前招的临时工比较多，人员流动性大，为了方便和其他同事交接，在这个岗位上的通通被称为「零食工」。
t_en: Oh. We had a large bunch of temps in the past and employee turnover rate was high. Everyone who worked in this position was known as Snack Vendor to facilitate the handing over procedures.

t_ch: 只是个工作用的称呼而已，你不必在意。
t_en: It's just a term of address we use at work. Don't mind it too much.

t_ch: 船长周边是什么？
t_en: What is the captain's merchandise?

t_ch: 就是以每一任船长的为主题的商品。像是以船长形象制作出的立体拼图、船长签名与半身像的钥匙扣、玩偶…诸如此类的小玩意儿。
t_en: They are goods themed around the presiding captain. Examples include 3D puzzles designed after the captain's appearance, captain's autographs, captain's half-body keychains, dolls, and other knick-knacks.

t_ch: 我是船长，就不能免费拿？
t_en: I'm the captain. Can't I get them for free?

t_ch: …啊，这个……商品每天都有财务部门来盘点的，您看看要不跟他们说一声？
t_en: ...Um, about that... Finance does an inventory check every day. Maybe you can check in with them?

t_ch: 我很抱歉，我只是个售货员，实在没有这个权利……
t_en: I'm really sorry, but I don't hold much authority as a mere salesperson...

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的！
t_en: Okay!

t_ch: 如果船长您还有什么需要…我就在这儿值班，随时喊我就好！
t_en: I'm on duty here, so just call for me if you need anything else, Captain!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，您忙您的去吧，不用管我！
t_en: Got it, feel free to go back to what you were busy with! Don't mind me!

t_ch: 深呼吸，稳住……
t_en: Deep breaths now, keep it together...

t_ch: 冷静，什么大场面我都见过，不能因为见到船长本人就这么激动。
t_en: Calm down. What haven't I seen before? I can't be getting this flustered just because I get to see captain in the flesh.

t_ch: 深呼吸…吸气…吐气…不愧是我，果然冷静点了，手都不抖了。
t_en: Deep breaths... Breathe in, and out... Good job, me! I've calmed down a little already. Even my hands have stopped shaking.

t_ch: 不得不说船长{F#她}{M#他}本人比玩偶更可爱…不知道能不能要个签名，有亲签的话这算不算全匹诺康尼最珍稀的限量款？。
t_en: Wow, captain does look much cuter IRL than {F#her}{M#his} doll... I wonder if I can get an autograph. Would it be the most priceless limited-edition merchandise in the whole of Penacony if captain signs it {F#herself}{M#himself}?

t_ch: …啊，船长是在看我吗？该不会以为我在摸鱼吧？
t_en: ...Eep, is captain looking at me? Does it look like I'm slacking off!?

t_ch: …吸气…吐气…冷静点，现在是上班时间…微笑，清嗓子…好，我果然受过专业训练的优秀售货员！
t_en: ...Breathe in, and out... Calm down, I'm at work now. *smile* Ahem. Alright, I'm a professionally-trained and outstanding salesperson!

t_ch: …是我的疏忽，都怪我。
t_en: ...I was careless. It's my fault.

t_ch: 不知道他们处理这单顾客投诉要多久…啊，我的好评率又掉了…我这个月的工资……
t_en: I wonder how long they'll take to process this complaint...? Oh no, my rating has fallen again! So will my salary this month...

t_ch: 哎，都怪我做事拖拉，一直没处理掉，这下可踢到铁板了……
t_en: *sigh* It's all because of my procrastination! I should've done it and got it out of the way. I've really gone and shot myself in the foot now...

t_ch: 被顾客当面喷了个狗血淋头…太丢人了，时间要是能倒流就好了，我真想回到那一瞬间给自己一巴掌……
t_en: How mortifying, I got chewed out by a customer... If I could turn back time, I won't hesitate to slap myself awake at that exact moment!

t_ch: …要是流程走到人力部门那边，他们会不会开除我？呜呜…我家里还指望我补贴下生活费呢…这下怎么办呐……
t_en: ...Will HR fire me if this reaches them? *sob* My family's expecting me to help out with our living expenses... What do I do now?

t_ch: 什么情况？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 别担心，船长来了。
t_en: Let the captain take your worries away.

t_ch: 那个…船长，我、我之前在工作上有些疏漏，没有及时处理之前的周边，导致客人原定的零食礼盒发错货了…发成了前任船长的周边。
t_en: Umm... captain, I—I failed to sort out the previous batch of merchandise in time because I was negligent. A customer received the previous captain's merchandise instead of the snack box they ordered.

t_ch: 顾客气炸了，就去投诉我发的货不对板，要求十倍赔偿…呜呜…您能不能跟人力那边说一声，千万别开除我，我会改的，我保证以后绝不再犯。
t_en: The customer blew up in rage and lodged a complaint against me for sending them things they did not order, demanding a tenfold compensation... *sob* D—Do you think you could ask HR not to fire me? I promise this will never ever happen again.

t_ch: …听起来是他在勒索。
t_en: ...It sounds like he's trying to blackmail you.

t_ch: 小问题，不至于开除…
t_en: It's only a small issue. You won't be fired.

t_ch: …真的吗？！
t_en: ...Really!?

t_ch: 谢谢船长！您这么说我就放心了！我会努力工作的，船长你真是个好人…呜呜……
t_en: Thank you, captain! It's a relief to hear that! I'll continue to work hard. Captain, you're such a nice person... *sob*

t_ch: 贩售船长周边是我们的传统，我的初衷只是想留住一些关于船长的美好回忆，没有想到会造成这样的误会……
t_en: Selling the captain's merchandise is our tradition. I only wanted to leave something to remember our last captain by. I didn't think it would cause so much trouble...

t_ch: 也怪我，我早该改改自己的拖延症了……
t_en: It's my fault too. I should've kept my procrastination in check before this...

t_ch: 搞成这个局面真的很抱歉…如果时间能倒流就好了……
t_en: I'm really sorry for causing so much trouble... If only I could turn back time...

t_ch: …但现在光这么说也没用，只能以后表现得好一些，吃一堑当长一智吧，大副说得对，还是要照流程行事才行。
t_en: ...All this talk of regret wouldn't change a thing anyway. I'll just have to work harder to make up for it. This is a lesson to learn from. The first mate is right, following proper procedures is the way to go.

t_ch: 我只是个小小的售货员！
t_en: I'm just a mere salesperson!

t_ch: 有些住客总是对我们的小店抱有不切实际的期待，这又不是艇上包间的餐厅，有大厨给你现做！说什么「卖得贵」，真的服了！
t_en: Why do some guests have such terribly unrealistic expectations of our store!? We're not a restaurant serving private cabins of the airship, nor do we have a chef to serve meals cooked-to-order! I can't believe they're actually complaining that our prices are high!

t_ch: 还说什么「天价周边」「不实用」…这可是空艇诶？运输成本不要钱吗？买周边什么时候要考虑实用了，这不都是纪念品吗？！
t_en: They find our merchandise overpriced and impractical... But hello? We're on an airship! Do they think shipping is free!? And when has practicality ever been a consideration when purchasing collectibles? Aren't they meant to be souvenirs!?

t_ch: 而且我只是个小小的零食工！跟我抱怨有用的话，我还会在这里当售货员吗？！
t_en: Plus, I'm just a lowly Snack Vendor! If complaining to me worked, would I still be working as a salesperson here!?

t_ch: 哎，这些住客看着都是非富即贵的，怎么买点东西都这么小家子气，真是一帮难伺候的主儿……
t_en: *sigh* These guests are either rich or filthy rich. Who would've expected them to turn into stingy misers when shopping! What a difficult bunch of customers...

t_ch: 能来空艇上工作是我的荣幸！
t_en: It's my honor to work on the airship!

t_ch: 虽然住客们很挑剔，但能在这儿工作，每天见到各种大人物，我还是挺幸运的！嘿嘿！
t_en: The customers may be demanding, but I get to meet all kinds of bigwigs at work every day. Heh, I guess I'm lucky to be able to work here!

t_ch: 说起来，现在换了新船长，空艇也要换名字吧？「晖长石号」要作废咯…不知道船长您会取个什么样的名字呢？
t_en: Actually, now that we have a new captain, are we going to rename the airship? Looks like Radiant Feldspar's going to be a thing of the past... What will the captain name it, though?

t_ch: 听说还要更换涂装什么的，这种事千古难寻，怎么都被我碰上了？
t_en: I heard they're also going to repaint the airship. Just my luck to be around when they have plans for such rare works.

t_ch: 未来还长着呢，我看您模样还挺年轻的，年纪轻轻的就事业有成，颜值又高，总感觉船长您以后会成为了不得的大人物……
t_en: Ah, you've got a long future ahead of you, and you look pretty young too. With impressive accomplishments at a young age and good looks to boot, I can't help but believe that you'll become someone legendary in the future, captain...

t_ch: 如果我能一直跟在您身边，给您打工…搞不好以后会被我遇到那种「见证历史的时刻」。四舍五入也算历史留名了吧？光是想想就让人兴奋！
t_en: If I'm lucky enough to continue working for you... I may even get the chance to witness a historic moment when it happens. I would've made my mark in history too, wouldn't I? Ooh, the thought alone is enough to excite me!

t_ch: 到了那个时候，我要告诉我的小孩：「看，妈妈在历史书上还留下过几笔呢！」
t_en: By that time, I'll tell my children, "Look, you can even find traces of mom in the history books!"

t_ch: …老实说我是为了生计才到这里调饮…如果口感不佳，还请见谅……
t_en: ...To be honest, I'm only concocting drinks to make ends meet. Please forgive me if it doesn't taste good...

t_ch: 你在嘀咕什么呢？
t_en: What are you muttering about?

t_ch: 上班时间说这个不妥吧…
t_en: Should you really be saying this at work...?

t_ch: 真的很抱歉。我刚学调饮没多久，怕技艺不熟练，给客人调饮时出错，所以提前练习一下道歉的话术。
t_en: I'm really sorry. I just got into drinksmithing and I doubt I'm good enough yet... I'm rehearsing my apologies in case I slip up and serve badly mixed drinks to the customers...

t_ch: 刚才没注意你在旁边，我是不是念得太大声吵到你了，抱歉抱歉，以后我会注意点的……
t_en: I didn't notice you were here. Was I too loud? I'm sorry, I'll pay more attention next time...

t_ch: 你是新手？
t_en: Are you a newbie?

t_ch: 你的技术很差吗？
t_en: Are your skills that bad?

t_ch: 我学调饮学了半年，师傅说我可以出师了，我想我的技术其实也没那么糟糕的。虽然学的时间比较短，但我多少有些基础……
t_en: I've trained for half a year before my mentor said I'm ready to work on my own. I guess I can craft drinks? I may not have trained for long, but I do know the basics...

t_ch: 我原本在一个乐队里弹贝斯，手上那点花活对我来说不算什么。就是…我从没来过这种高档地方，更别说工作了…难免有些紧张。
t_en: I used to be the bassist of a band, so it's no sweat entertaining them. It's just... I've never been anywhere this boujee, much less worked at one. I can't help getting the jitters.

t_ch: 跟我介绍下你的乐队吧。
t_en: Tell me about your band.

t_ch: 没啥好说的，乐队没有人气，作品也卖不出去。队友都是穷哥们，吃饭都是有一顿没一顿的。
t_en: There's nothing much to talk about, really. Our band isn't popular, nor does our music sell. Everyone in the band's so strapped for cash that they had to stretch out their meals.

t_ch: 怎么就突然转行了？
t_en: Why the sudden job switch?

t_ch: 搞音乐不赚钱啊，连吃饭的钱都没有……
t_en: Music wasn't making me any money. I wouldn't have survived otherwise...

t_ch: 这些年来，我的音乐事业没有任何发展，出碟录音样样都要花钱，还不如来这里调饮。
t_en: My music wasn't going anywhere those years, and you had to put in money to release albums, record tracks, and whatnot. Surely I'm better off here mixing drinks.

t_ch: 咳咳…现在是营业时间，我们还是聊点别的吧。那、那个！调饮师达夫竭诚为您服务！
t_en: Ahem, maybe we should talk about something else. I'm getting paid for this, after all... Uh, h—how are you doing? Drinksmith Dahf's at your service!

t_ch: 你这儿都卖些什么？
t_en: What have you got for sale here?

t_ch: 好的，那回头再聊，我也得继续工作了。
t_en: Alright, talk to you later then. I should get back to work too.

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I want to ask...

t_ch: 尽管问吧，我眼下还算有空，想要随便聊聊的无任欢迎——不过说归说，你可别去投诉我……
t_en: Ask away. I'm free at the moment, so I'm all ears if you wanna chat — as long as you don't tattle on me...

t_ch: 能给我说说你的故事吗？
t_en: So, tell me about your story?

t_ch: 故事？我没什么故事啊，我就是个普通…呃，一个小乐队的贝斯手。乐队也没什么人气，作品也一般，赚不到钱，所以我就出来找工作了。
t_en: Story? I ain't got no story. I'm just an average... uh, bassist of a small band. A nobody band with mediocre music that wasn't making any money. That's why I left for another job.

t_ch: 以前倒是来应聘过「晖长石号」的表演乐手，结果连简历都没过。后来遇到一位老师，说我手指灵活有力适合调饮…接着我就来空艇上干活了。
t_en: I previously applied to be a musician on the Radiant Feldspar, but they didn't even take a second glance at my resume. Then I met someone who found my hands dexterous enough to craft drinks... so here I am, working on the airship.

t_ch: 你对现在的工作不满意吗？
t_en: Are you unhappy with your current job?

t_ch: 嗯？你这说话的语气怎么这么像我老板？
t_en: Hmm? Why do you sound like my boss all of a sudden?

t_ch: 我不是不满意，只是觉得有点无语——我以前想来空艇上当乐师驻场表演，结果现在成了调饮师……
t_en: Well, I'm not exactly unhappy. I guess I just don't know what to feel... I mean, look, I wanted to be the airship's resident musician, but I ended up as a drinksmith here...

t_ch: 那你有考虑转行吗？
t_en: Have you thought of going into a new job?

t_ch: 那倒没有。其实调饮让我发现了一种新的艺术形式，我对节奏的感觉和手指的灵活性，在调饮时得到了新的应用。这或许是音乐给我的另一种礼物。但是……
t_en: That, I haven't. You know, I realize mixing drinks is a whole new way of looking at art. My sense of rhythm and dexterous fingers come together differently when crafting drinks. Maybe this is music's blessing for me, but...

t_ch: 但是我们乐队的吉他手说：「你摇冰块的声音比你的琴音响得多。」哎，差点把我气到昏迷。可现在我想通了，成年人为了谋生拉下脸很正常，这些闲言碎语算什么。
t_en: But when our guitarist said my cocktail shakers resound a lot better than my bass... Boy, I almost went into a stroke. *sigh* I've thought it through now. Pride doesn't pay the bills, so what's a little mindless when I'm earning my keep.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，那回头再聊，我也得继续工作了。
t_en: Alright, talk to you later then. I should get back to work too.

t_ch: 我想明白了，到我这个年纪，没天赋没钱的就别捣鼓音乐了，还是趁早转行吧。
t_en: I've figured it out. At my age, being penniless and with no talent to boot, a path in music probably isn't meant to be. I'm better off jumping ship early.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，那回头再聊，我也得继续工作了。
t_en: Alright, talk to you later then. I should get back to work too.

t_ch: 达夫摇晃杯身的动作流畅而自然，以至于让周遭的喧嚣都变得遥远。
t_en: Dahf moves effortlessly with his shaker, as though part of an orchestrated dance, making everything else in the surrounding fade into oblivion.

t_ch: 我是个靠手吃饭的手艺人——字面意义。
t_en: I make a living with my own hands — I mean that literally.

t_ch: 弹琴的那点手艺没有让我变成大明星，却让我成为了调饮师，给了我一份稳定的工作。
t_en: The skill I honed from playing the bass didn't turn me into a superstar, but instead, landed me a stable job as a drinksmith.

t_ch: 或许不是每个人都能理解，但我觉得或许这就是成年人的自我和解吧。打工也没什么不好的，至少我不会再为生活费发愁了。
t_en: Some may disagree, but I guess this is just coming to terms with adult life. There's nothing bad about having a job, at least I never have to worry about making ends meet anymore.

t_ch: 你见达夫突然放下手中的搅拌杆，长长地叹了口气，眉间似乎有些阴郁。
t_en: Dahf abruptly releases his grip over the stirrer and you see him let out a long sigh, his expression turning somewhat gloomy.

t_ch: …感觉我活得真窝囊，人到中年，一事无成……
t_en: ...I feel like an utter loser in life. I've lived half of my life without accomplishing anything...

t_ch: 其实我几年前已经有预感，我这辈子就会这么蹉跎下去。搞乐队十几年了，我自己的作曲能力怎么样我是再清楚不过了……
t_en: To be honest, I already had a hunch several years back. I knew I'd continue wasting my life away. I've played in a band for over a decade, I know full well how my compositions fare...

t_ch: 「没天分就别学人玩音乐！」哎，那个乐评人说得真对……
t_en: "Don't jump onto the music bandwagon when you obviously lack the talent!" *sigh* That music critic was bang on...

t_ch: 家里人也天天骂我，这些年来我连个礼物钱都出不起，时不时还要靠父母打钱周转…如果我早点转行，我是不是就会拥有一个相对成功的人生了？
t_en: My family berated me plenty, too. I couldn't even afford to get them gifts for years, and I—I even had to ask them for living allowance every now and then... Would I have led a better life if I went into a new job sooner?

t_ch: 都怪我…执迷不悟……
t_en: It's all my fault... Why was I so stubborn...?

t_ch: 你可以工作生活两不误啊。
t_en: But you can work and pursue your interests.

t_ch: 只要你想，什么时候都不算晚。
t_en: It's never too late as long as you put your mind to it.

t_ch: …你说得有点道理。
t_en: ...You have a point.

t_ch: 其实我心里也清楚，刚玩乐队的那一年…是我人生中最快乐的一年。
t_en: Deep down, I know that too. I was the happiest during my first year in the band.

t_ch: …就这么抛弃过去的自己，我…我还是舍不得……
t_en: I—I can't bring myself to close the chapter on my past just like that...

t_ch: …算起来，玩音乐整整十年了，坚持了这么久真的要放弃吗？艾克索说得对，把速弹的臂力浪费在摇晃水杯上，真是浪费。
t_en: ...Thinking back now, I've already been making music for a good ten years. Should I really be giving up on it after holding out this far? Axel's right, it's a complete waste busting my guns on these shakers instead of shredding the bass.

t_ch: 如果这样的生活继续下去，我还有空弹贝斯吗？如果继续这样，我会不会有天连怎么弹琴都忘了？这里也没有练习的地方，员工休息区虽大，却容不下我的箱头和箱体。
t_en: Will I even get the time to play the bass if I keep up with this job? Will I forget how to play the bass one day? There's no place for me to practice here. The staff lounge may be spacious, but it's not meant for my amp head and cab.

t_ch: …孤独的人在音乐里逃避现实，那我算不算在现实里逃避梦想呢？
t_en: ...People turn to music to drown out solitude from the real world. So, am I escaping my dreams to go back to reality?

t_ch: 生活不应该是这样的…我小时候可没想过会有这么一天，长大了就变成工作的奴隶，怎么想都觉得可悲……
t_en: My life shouldn't play out this way... Even as a child, I never ever imagined growing up to become a slave to my job. This is pathetic, through and through...

t_ch: …真是够了，又来一单！
t_en: ...I've had enough! Why do the orders keep coming!?

t_ch: 制冰机又坏了，这一地的水，调完这一杯还要拖地…我到底是调饮师还是清洁工？
t_en: It's flooding in here because the ice machine's down again. Now I have to mop the floor after crafting this drink... Am I really a drinksmith? Or a cleaner?

t_ch: 天天站在这里脚都麻了，手指泡在水里皮都皱了，又冷又痛，还要天天笑脸相迎。我到底是做错了什么要天天来上刑？
t_en: My feet's numb from standing all day and my fingers get wrinkly from being in contact with water. But hey, I'm supposed to ignore the cold and pain, and greet everyone with a smile on my face. What have I done, really, to be subjected to this torture called work?

t_ch: …我本该站在舞台上发光发热的，现在却困在这里…上班就是上刑，我明白了…什么时候我才能被无罪释放啊？！
t_en: ...I was meant to dazzle on stage, but here I am, trapped... I now know that having to work is punishment in itself. Gah, when can I be free from this misery!?

t_ch: 即使是繁忙的工作时间，达夫的脸上也总是挂着笑容。
t_en: Dahf always wears a smile on his face, no matter how busy his work gets.

t_ch: 调饮和弹琴差不多，都是手工活儿，他人在品尝后露出满足的表情就是最大的满足…都是对我指头灵活性与手臂稳定性的认可。
t_en: Mixing drinks and playing the bass are no different. I work the magic with my hands. What truly makes me happy is the glow of satisfaction on their faces after tasting my drinks... That's all the approval these dexterous digits of mine need.

t_ch: 上次那个来找我调饮的叫什么来着？…杰克？好像就是哪家唱片公司的老总？不知道找他签约分成能不能高点……
t_en: The guy that ordered a drink a while ago? What's his name? Jack? He seems to be the CEO of a record label. Will he give me a good cut if I sign with him...?

t_ch: 这样的生活也挺好的，包吃包住有工资还有小费，这样的工作上哪里找？
t_en: I quite enjoy this life now. I get a place to stay, free meals, a salary, and even tips to boot. Where else can I find such a good job?

t_ch: 平平淡淡才是真呐……
t_en: The best life is indeed a simple and mundane one...

t_ch: 集中注意力，别让我发现你们中有人在偷懒！记住，在这艘空艇上，每个人都必须全力以赴，包括我在内！从现在起，我希望看到的是效率和纪律。
t_en: Stay focused, and don't let me catch any of you slacking off! Remember that everyone on this ship, including me, must give their all! From now on, I want you to show me efficiency and discipline.

t_ch: 把你的脚背绷紧点，注意队形！还有你的扣子怎么扣错了？这点小事都做不好？
t_en: Keep your insteps tight and pay attention to the formation! Why are your buttons wrong? Can't you even do something this simple?

t_ch: 记住，这里是旅游胜地，但我们不是游客！…呃，船长…您来啦？！
t_en: Remember, this may be a tourist destination, but we're not the tourists! ...Ah, captain! When did you arrive?

t_ch: 不用这么在意形象细节吧？
t_en: Is there a need to be so hung up over such minor grooming details?

t_ch: 对下属还是别太严苛了。
t_en: You don't have to be so harsh on your subordinates.

t_ch: 请您放心，我只是在训练的时候管得比较严，私底下我们关系还是挺好。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm only this strict during training. We actually get along well in private.

t_ch: 我之所以注重这些细节，是因为…形象工作是基础中的基础。一个团队要想被尊重，首先就要从外在形象做起。
t_en: I'm particular about such details because maintaining one's image is one of the most basic things to always be mindful of. If a team wants to be respected, they must start by working on how they present themselves.

t_ch: 所以我坚持为船员配备统一的制服并进行队形训练。这不仅能提升他们的专业形象，也能增强团队凝聚力。
t_en: That is why I insist on making the crew wear uniforms and practice standing in formation. This enhances their professional image and their cohesiveness as a team.

t_ch: 我刚才在培训新招的船员，没注意到您在后面，非常抱歉。请问您找我是有什么事吗？
t_en: My apologies, I was training some newly recruited crew members just now and didn't notice you behind me. Is there something I can do for you?

t_ch: 没事，我随便逛逛。
t_en: Nah. I was just taking a walk around the place.

t_ch: 好的，船长您请便。
t_en: I see. Please enjoy your walk, captain.

t_ch: 我来视察下你的工作。
t_en: I'm here for an inspection.

t_ch: 这是我的荣幸。
t_en: I'm honored.

t_ch: 船长，如果您发现了我们服务中的不足，请随时告诉我。我们致力于为每一位宾客——包括您——提供最完美的体验。
t_en: If you notice anything about our service that needs improvement, please let me know anytime, captain. We're committed to providing the best experience for every guest, including you.

t_ch: 集中注意力，别让我发现你们中有人在偷懒！看到没，船长来了，大家要好好表现，否则…哼哼！
t_en: Stay focused, and don't let me catch any of you slacking off! Do you see that? The captain is here! Do your best, everyone! Or else... Hmph!

t_ch: 船长，您找我有什么事吗？
t_en: Captain, is there something I can do for you?

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: There's something I want to know...

t_ch: 您说吧，我听着呢。
t_en: Please go on. I'm listening.

t_ch: 向我汇报下你的工作计划。
t_en: Give me a report of your work plan.

t_ch: 我正在检查大家的工作细节，因为我认为每一个细节都可能影响整体的工作效率，这会直接影响我推进后续的工作。
t_en: I'm currently going over the details of everyone's work. All these details have the potential to influence our overall efficiency, which directly affects subsequent work progress.

t_ch: 如果一切顺利，接下来我将对空艇上的工作流程进行优化，确保每一项任务都能在最短时间内完成，同时保证质量。
t_en: If everything goes well, I'll start optimizing work processes on the airship to ensure every task is duly completed within the shortest time possible.

t_ch: 请您放心，我已经以住客的视角完整体验过空艇上的所有设施，所以我很清楚目前住客们的需求。
t_en: Rest assured that I've already experienced all the facilities on this airship from a guest's perspective, so I daresay I have an excellent understanding of their current needs.

t_ch: 住客们有什么反馈吗？
t_en: Any feedback from the guests?

t_ch: 对于住客们的反馈，我已经详细记录在案并一一分析过了。
t_en: I've already recorded their feedback in detail and analyzed it.

t_ch: 目前所有住客对我们的服务表示非常满意，尤其是对我们船员的专业度和服务态度给予了高度评价。但无论成绩有多好，肯定还有一些改进的空间。
t_en: All the guests are currently very satisfied with our service. In particular, they have nothing but praises for the crew's professionalism and service. Even so, there's always room for improvement.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 说实话，感谢船长您对我的信任。相信您也看到了，目前空艇上的每一项运作都还有进步的空间，我会继续努力的！
t_en: If I may be honest, I'd like to thank you for showing such trust in me, Captain. As you may already be aware, there's still room for further enhancements for everything on this airship. I'll keep doing my best!

t_ch: 没什么事的话我就继续工作啦——诶，趁我和船长聊天，你们在干什么呢？想偷懒？你们不会犯那种愚蠢的错误，对吧？
t_en: If there's nothing else, I'll get back to work... Hey! What do you think you're doing while I'm reporting matters to the captain? Slacking off? You wouldn't do anything as silly as that, right?

t_ch: 我想给空艇更名并换涂装。
t_en: I want to rename the airship and change her livery.

t_ch: 好的，稍等，等我检查下预案——根据我们的预算，目前只支持原储备的三种方案。方案详情如下：
t_en: Okay, please wait a moment while I check the proposals. Hm, our current budget can only support three predetermined designs:

t_ch: 第一种是命名为「开拓之尾号」，尾翼配以星穹列车列车长帕姆模样的涂装。
t_en: The first is the Trailblaze's Stern. It features an insignia designed after Pom-Pom, the conductor of the Astral Express, on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 第二种是命名为「塔塔洛夫号」，尾翼配以垃圾之王塔塔洛夫模样的涂装。
t_en: The second is The Tatalov. It features an insignia designed after Tatalov the Garbage King on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 第三种是命名为「飞翔时针号」，尾翼配以钟表小子模样的涂装。
t_en: The third is The Soaring Clock Hand. It features an insignia designed after Clockie on the tail of the airship.

t_ch: 您想选哪一种呢？
t_en: Which would you prefer?

t_ch: 我选帕姆！
t_en: I'll go with Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 我选塔塔洛夫！
t_en: I'll go with Tatalov!

t_ch: 我选钟表小子！
t_en: I'll go with Clockie!

t_ch: 好的，请你稍等，我就吩咐人去改。
t_en: Certainly. One moment, please. I'll have someone change it right this instant.

t_ch: 罗莎按照你的命令，派人更改了空艇的命名与尾翼的涂装。
t_en: Rosa sends staff members to rename the airship and change her tail livery as you ordered.

t_ch: 如您所愿，已经更改成功了，如果您还想继续更换方案，随时喊我就好。
t_en: I've made the desired changes. Please let me know whenever you'd like to change the design again.

t_ch: 没事，你继续忙你的吧。
t_en: It's nothing. You can return to what you were doing.

t_ch: 好的，船长辛苦了！
t_en: Understood. Thank you, Captain!

t_ch: 很高兴见到您，船长。在这里，没有生物是完美的，我也不例外。但每一次航行都是一次成长的机会，我相信在您的带领下，我们会变得更好——我以能成为您的大副为荣。
t_en: Nice to meet you, Captain. You'll find that none of the lifeforms here are perfect, myself included. Nevertheless, every flight is an opportunity to grow. With you leading us, I'm sure we'll only keep improving and getting better. I'm honored to be your first mate.

t_ch: 船长～愿您与其他同伴都能在未来的航行中找到自己的价值和位置。
t_en: May you and your companions find self-worth and a place to belong to in future flights, Captain.

t_ch: 冷芯，这是你新招的人吗？
t_en: Caldwick, is this the person you just recruited?

t_ch: 是的，经过我的严格筛选，我将他从一家餐厅的后厨招了进来。尽管过往经验不匹配，但他的天赋很好，我相信他很快就会成为我们不可或缺的一员。
t_en: Yes, after a strict screening process, I recruited him from the kitchen of a restaurant. Although he doesn't have the required experience, he's a gifted one. I am certain he will soon become an indispensable member of our team.

t_ch: 谢谢夸奖，非常感谢大副您对我的栽培！
t_en: Thank you for the compliment! I am grateful for all the training you've given me!

t_ch: 嘴巴还挺甜的…
t_en: You say such nice things...

t_ch: 嘿嘿～我只是说了我的心里话。大副鼓励我要发挥自己的特长，没事就勤说话。所以我都是心里想到什么就说什么，尽量不让嘴巴闲着。
t_en: Hehe, I'm just saying whatever comes to mind. The first mate always encourages me to show off my strengths and talk more when I have nothing to do. That's why I always say whatever comes to mind and try not to let my tongue be idle.

t_ch: 新招的这批船员，他们的过往经验与技能看起来和招聘需求并不匹配。但我认为，每个生物都有其独特的潜力与价值，通过传统的途径是无法衡量的。
t_en: While the professional experience and abilities of the newly recruited crew members don't seem to meet the hiring criteria, it is my belief that every living thing has their own unique potential and value. These things can't be measured through traditional means.

t_ch: 纵观空艇人员现况，我们缺的并不是技能如何娴熟的侍从，而是那些热心且更懂得珍惜机会的船员。
t_en: Looking at the current situation of the airship staff, what we lack isn't skilled attendants, but enthusiastic crew members who cherish the opportunities they are given.

t_ch: 「并不匹配」的意思是？
t_en: What do you mean when you say they don't seem to meet the hiring criteria?

t_ch: 个人从业经历与专业技能，与「晖长石号」过往招聘船员的要求并不一致，甚至彼此矛盾。
t_en: It means that their professional experience and abilities don't match — or may even contradict — past requirements for recruiting crew members of the Radiant Feldspar.

t_ch: 听起来蛮深奥的…
t_en: That sounds kind of deep...

t_ch: 世事洞明皆学问，我目前所掌握的也只是一些管理学知识的皮毛，离「深奥」这个层级还差得远呢。
t_en: To have insight into worldly affairs is to be truly learned. I only know the basics of management and am nowhere near having a deep understanding of it.

t_ch: 你正想与冷芯就他的人才管理知识继续探讨下去，但在你正要开口的瞬间，你却发现——
t_en: You want to keep discussing the art of talent management with Caldwick, but just as you are about to speak, you notice that...
